    • Enrollment & Dropout Modeling
    • Examples
    • Example 1: Two arm | Fixed design | Continuous
    • Example 2: Two arm | Interim | Continuous | t-test
    • Example 3: Two arm | Fixed design | Two correlated continuous endpoints | t-test
    • Example 4: Two arm | Fixed design | Correlated continuous & time-to-event endpoints | t-test + survival analysis
    • Example 5: Multi-arm | Fixed design | Single continuous endpoint | Dunnett test + MCP-Mod
    • Example 6: Two-arm | Fixed design | Single continuous endpoint | Overall + exploratory subgroup analysis
    • Example 7: Two-arm | Fixed design | Single continuous endpoint | Bayesian Go/No-Go with placebo borrowing
    • Example 8: Seamless Ph2a/2b | Two-arm -> Multi-arm | Single continuous endpoint | Bayesian Go/No-Go + Bayesian MCP-Mod
    • Example Multi Run: Two arm | Interim | Continuous | t-test

Authors and Citation

Authors

  • Matthew Valko. Author.

  • Saumil Shah. Author, maintainer.

  • Ming Yang. Contributor.

  • Mitchell Thomann. Contributor.

  • Boehringer Ingelheim Pharma GmbH & Co. KG. Copyright holder, funder.

Citation

Valko M, Shah S (2026). rxsim: Reducing friction for Randomized Clinical Trial simulations. R package version 0.1.2.9000, https://boehringer-ingelheim.github.io/rxsim. 

@Manual{,
  title = {rxsim: Reducing friction for Randomized Clinical Trial simulations},
  author = {Matthew Valko and Saumil Shah},
  year = {2026},
  note = {R package version 0.1.2.9000},
